note: Round 6 = attacking the Round-5 write-path floor two ways. (A) A kernel no-subscriber fast-path in src/core/state.js (the set trap skips all notify/flush work when a store has zero listeners + no beforeFlush hooks). (B) A `plain` lab mode: pull renderer over a plain array, no state() at all — the theoretical floor. Metric that matters: maxProducerMs (`prod`) = worst synchronous write-pass frame; it is a direct duration of the 3000-cell write loop, far cleaner than input-delay (paint-dominated) or worstBlock (throttle-inflated). All runs: 20x CPU throttle, dot off, 3000 cells, continuous typing, measure 6000ms. Throttle drifts per case (shown); prod is throttle-scaled, so read the ~10x within-metric jumps, not the exact ms.

A_kernel_fast_path.change: src/core/state.js — listenerCount tracked in addListener; set trap returns after storing the value when listenerCount===0 && beforeFlushHooks.length===0; notifySubscribers early-returns when listenerCount===0. 488 existing tests pass unchanged (behaviourally transparent).

dashboard.note: ~2x; only 8% of cells write per frame so the pass is smaller

input_median_ms_noisy.caveat: Directionally down but within run-to-run noise: OFF (which never takes the fast path) swung 5875->2536 storm between runs from throttle/GC alone. Input is dominated by DOM paint under throttle, not the write pass; treat prod + profile as the clean signals.

B_plain_data_pull_layer.change: examples/render-queue-lab/main.js — new `plain` mode: high-churn layer held in a plain array, producer writes it directly (no proxy/set/notify/flush/batch), scheduling byte-identical to `pull`.

cpu_profile_plain_storm_3000_20x.kernel_write_path: 0% (no set / notifySubscribers / state.js get anywhere in the profile; the residual batch/flush samples are the once-per-second ui readout store)
cpu_profile_plain_storm_3000_20x.note: The plain array write is the theoretical floor — the write layer contributes essentially nothing; the frame is all DOM paint.

verdict.kernel_write_path_progression_pull_storm: ~9% (before) -> ~1.5% (fast path) -> ~0% (plain). The fast path captures ~85% of the available kernel-write savings while keeping the FULL reactive API; plain captures the last sliver by abandoning reactivity.
verdict.pull_vs_plain_gap: After the fast path, pull's write pass (~16 ms storm / ~14 ms dashboard) sits within ~1-2x of a raw plain-array write (~16 ms / ~8 ms). The residual is the Proxy set trap itself (Object.is + brand + dispatch) — real but small, and swamped by DOM paint.
verdict.so_what: The no-subscriber fast path is the high-leverage, shippable win: it makes an unsubscribed store's write nearly free, so a pull/streaming renderer does NOT need to abandon state() for the high-churn layer. A plain-data layer is the true floor but its marginal gain over a fast-path'd unsubscribed store is small on an already-small write pass. Neither raises the fps ceiling (that is still content-visibility / less work) or fixes input under storm alone (paint-bound); they remove the write-pass floor that Round 5 identified.
